lines.gurmukhi: ਰਾਗੁ ਭੈਰਉ ਮਹਲਾ ੫ ਪੜਤਾਲ ਘਰੁ ੩ ॥
lines.translit: raag bhairau mahalaa 5 parrataal ghar 3 |
lines.english: Raag Bhairao, Fifth Mehl, Partaal, Third House:

lines.gurmukhi: ੴ ਸਤਿਗੁਰ ਪ੍ਰਸਾਦਿ ॥
lines.translit: ik oankaar satigur prasaad |
lines.english: One Universal Creator God. By The Grace Of The True Guru:

lines.gurmukhi: ਪਰਤਿਪਾਲ ਪ੍ਰਭ ਕ੍ਰਿਪਾਲ ਕਵਨ ਗੁਨ ਗਨੀ ॥
lines.translit: paratipaal prabh kripaal kavan gun ganee |
lines.english: God is the Compassionate Cherisher. Who can count His Glorious Virtues?

lines.gurmukhi: ਅਨਿਕ ਰੰਗ ਬਹੁ ਤਰੰਗ ਸਰਬ ਕੋ ਧਨੀ ॥੧॥ ਰਹਾਉ ॥
lines.translit: anik rang bahu tarang sarab ko dhanee |1| rahaau |
lines.english: Countless colors, and countless waves of joy; He is the Master of all. ||1||Pause||

lines.gurmukhi: ਅਨਿਕ ਗਿਆਨ ਅਨਿਕ ਧਿਆਨ ਅਨਿਕ ਜਾਪ ਜਾਪ ਤਾਪ ॥
lines.translit: anik giaan anik dhiaan anik jaap jaap taap |
lines.english: Endless spiritual wisdom, endless meditations, endless chants, intense meditations and austere self-disciplines.

lines.gurmukhi: ਅਨਿਕ ਗੁਨਿਤ ਧੁਨਿਤ ਲਲਿਤ ਅਨਿਕ ਧਾਰ ਮੁਨੀ ॥੧॥
lines.translit: anik gunit dhunit lalit anik dhaar munee |1|
lines.english: Countless virtues, musical notes and playful sports; countless silent sages enshrine Him in their hearts. ||1||

lines.gurmukhi: ਅਨਿਕ ਨਾਦ ਅਨਿਕ ਬਾਜ ਨਿਮਖ ਨਿਮਖ ਅਨਿਕ ਸ੍ਵਾਦ ਅਨਿਕ ਦੋਖ ਅਨਿਕ ਰੋਗ ਮਿਟਹਿ ਜਸ ਸੁਨੀ ॥
lines.translit: anik naad anik baaj nimakh nimakh anik svaad anik dokh anik rog mitteh jas sunee |
lines.english: Countless melodies, countless instruments, countless tastes, each and every instant. Countless mistakes and countless diseases are removed by hearing His Praise.

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾਨਕ ਸੇਵ ਅਪਾਰ ਦੇਵ ਤਟਹ ਖਟਹ ਬਰਤ ਪੂਜਾ ਗਵਨ ਭਵਨ ਜਾਤ੍ਰ ਕਰਨ ਸਗਲ ਫਲ ਪੁਨੀ ॥੨॥੧॥੫੭॥੮॥੨੧॥੭॥੫੭॥੯੩॥
lines.translit: naanak sev apaar dev tattah khattah barat poojaa gavan bhavan jaatr karan sagal fal punee |2|1|57|8|21|7|57|93|
lines.english: O Nanak, serving the Infinite, Divine Lord, one earns all the rewards and merits of performing the six rituals, fasts, worship services, pilgrimages to sacred rivers, and journeys to sacred shrines. ||2||1||57||8||21||7||57||93||

lines.gurmukhi: ੴ ਸਤਿਗੁਰ ਪ੍ਰਸਾਦਿ ॥
lines.translit: ik oankaar satigur prasaad |
lines.english: One Universal Creator God. By The Grace Of The True Guru:

lines.gurmukhi: ਭੈਰਉ ਅਸਟਪਦੀਆ ਮਹਲਾ ੧ ਘਰੁ ੨ ॥
lines.translit: bhairau asattapadeea mahalaa 1 ghar 2 |
lines.english: Bhairao, Ashtpadheeyaa, First Mehl, Second House:

lines.gurmukhi: ਆਤਮ ਮਹਿ ਰਾਮੁ ਰਾਮ ਮਹਿ ਆਤਮੁ ਚੀਨਸਿ ਗੁਰ ਬੀਚਾਰਾ ॥
lines.translit: aatam meh raam raam meh aatam cheenas gur beechaaraa |
lines.english: The Lord is in the soul, and the soul is in the Lord. This is realized through the Guru's Teachings.

lines.gurmukhi: ਅੰਮ੍ਰਿਤ ਬਾਣੀ ਸਬਦਿ ਪਛਾਣੀ ਦੁਖ ਕਾਟੈ ਹਉ ਮਾਰਾ ॥੧॥
lines.translit: amrit baanee sabad pachhaanee dukh kaattai hau maaraa |1|
lines.english: The Ambrosial Word of the Guru's Bani is realized through the Word of the Shabad. Sorrow is dispelled, and egotism is eliminated. ||1||

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਹ ਦੇਖਾਂ ਤਹ ਏਕਾ ਬੇਦਨ ਆਪੇ ਬਖਸੈ ਸਬਦਿ ਧੁਰੇ ॥੧॥ ਰਹਾਉ ॥
lines.translit: jeh dekhaan teh ekaa bedan aape bakhasai sabad dhure |1| rahaau |
lines.english: Wherever I look, I see the pain of the same disease. The Primal Lord Himself bestows the Shabad of His Word. ||1||Pause||

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾਨਕ ਹਉਮੈ ਰੋਗ ਬੁਰੇ ॥
lines.translit: naanak haumai rog bure |
lines.english: O Nanak, the disease of egotism is so very deadly.

lines.gurmukhi: ਆਪੇ ਪਰਖੇ ਪਰਖਣਹਾਰੈ ਬਹੁਰਿ ਸੂਲਾਕੁ ਨ ਹੋਈ ॥
lines.translit: aape parakhe parakhanahaarai bahur soolaak na hoee |
lines.english: When the Appraiser Himself appraises the mortal, then he is not tested again.

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਿਨ ਕਉ ਨਦਰਿ ਭਈ ਗੁਰਿ ਮੇਲੇ ਪ੍ਰਭ ਭਾਣਾ ਸਚੁ ਸੋਈ ॥੨॥
lines.translit: jin kau nadar bhee gur mele prabh bhaanaa sach soee |2|
lines.english: Those who are blessed with His Grace meet with the Guru. They alone are true, who are pleasing to God. ||2||

lines.gurmukhi: ਮਾਤ ਪਿਤਾ ਮਾਇਆ ਦੇਹ ਸਿ ਰੋਗੀ ਰੋਗੀ ਕੁਟੰਬ ਸੰਜੋਗੀ ॥੩॥
lines.translit: maat pitaa maaeaa deh si rogee rogee kuttanb sanjogee |3|
lines.english: Mother, father, Maya and the body are diseased; those united with their relatives are diseased. ||3||

lines.gurmukhi: ਪਉਣੁ ਪਾਣੀ ਬੈਸੰਤਰੁ ਰੋਗੀ ਰੋਗੀ ਧਰਤਿ ਸਭੋਗੀ ॥
lines.translit: paun paanee baisantar rogee rogee dharat sabhogee |
lines.english: Air, water and fire are diseased; the world with its enjoyments is diseased.

lines.gurmukhi: ਹਰਿ ਪਦੁ ਚੀਨਿ ਭਏ ਸੇ ਮੁਕਤੇ ਗੁਰ ਕਾ ਸਬਦੁ ਵੀਚਾਰਾ ॥੪॥
lines.translit: har pad cheen bhe se mukate gur kaa sabad veechaaraa |4|
lines.english: Those who remember the Lord's Feet and contemplate the Word of the Guru's Shabad are liberated. ||4||

lines.gurmukhi: ਰੋਗੀ ਬ੍ਰਹਮਾ ਬਿਸਨੁ ਸਰੁਦ੍ਰਾ ਰੋਗੀ ਸਗਲ ਸੰਸਾਰਾ ॥
lines.translit: rogee brahamaa bisan sarudraa rogee sagal sansaaraa |
lines.english: Brahma, Vishnu and Shiva are diseased; the whole world is diseased.

lines.gurmukhi: ਰੋਗੀ ਸਾਤ ਸਮੁੰਦ ਸਨਦੀਆ ਖੰਡ ਪਤਾਲ ਸਿ ਰੋਗਿ ਭਰੇ ॥
lines.translit: rogee saat samund sanadeea khandd pataal si rog bhare |
lines.english: The seven seas are diseased, along with the rivers; the continents and the nether regions of the underworlds are full of disease.

lines.gurmukhi: ਹਰਿ ਕੇ ਲੋਕ ਸਿ ਸਾਚਿ ਸੁਹੇਲੇ ਸਰਬੀ ਥਾਈ ਨਦਰਿ ਕਰੇ ॥੫॥
lines.translit: har ke lok si saach suhele sarabee thaaee nadar kare |5|
lines.english: The people of the Lord dwell in Truth and peace; He blesses them with His Grace everywhere. ||5||

lines.gurmukhi: ਰੋਗੀ ਖਟ ਦਰਸਨ ਭੇਖਧਾਰੀ ਨਾਨਾ ਹਠੀ ਅਨੇਕਾ ॥
lines.translit: rogee khatt darasan bhekhadhaaree naanaa hatthee anekaa |
lines.english: The six Shaastras are diseased, as are the many who follow the different religious orders.

lines.gurmukhi: ਬੇਦ ਕਤੇਬ ਕਰਹਿ ਕਹ ਬਪੁਰੇ ਨਹ ਬੂਝਹਿ ਇਕ ਏਕਾ ॥੬॥
lines.translit: bed kateb kareh keh bapure neh boojheh ik ekaa |6|
lines.english: What can the poor Vedas and Bibles do? People do not understand the One and Only Lord. ||6||

lines.gurmukhi: ਮਿਠ ਰਸੁ ਖਾਇ ਸੁ ਰੋਗਿ ਭਰੀਜੈ ਕੰਦ ਮੂਲਿ ਸੁਖੁ ਨਾਹੀ ॥
lines.translit: mitth ras khaae su rog bhareejai kand mool sukh naahee |
lines.english: Eating sweet treats, the mortal is filled with disease; he finds no peace at all.

lines.gurmukhi: ਤੀਰਥਿ ਭਰਮੈ ਰੋਗੁ ਨ ਛੂਟਸਿ ਪੜਿਆ ਬਾਦੁ ਬਿਬਾਦੁ ਭਇਆ ॥
lines.translit: teerath bharamai rog na chhoottas parriaa baad bibaad bheaa |
lines.english: Wandering around at sacred shrines of pilgrimage, the mortal is not cured of his disease. Reading scripture, he gets involved in useless arguments.

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾਮੁ ਵਿਸਾਰਿ ਚਲਹਿ ਅਨ ਮਾਰਗਿ ਅੰਤ ਕਾਲਿ ਪਛੁਤਾਹੀ ॥੭॥
lines.translit: naam visaar chaleh an maarag ant kaal pachhutaahee |7|
lines.english: Forgetting the Naam, the Name of the Lord, they walk on other paths, and at the very last moment, they regret and repent. ||7||

lines.gurmukhi: ਦੁਬਿਧਾ ਰੋਗੁ ਸੁ ਅਧਿਕ ਵਡੇਰਾ ਮਾਇਆ ਕਾ ਮੁਹਤਾਜੁ ਭਇਆ ॥੮॥
lines.translit: dubidhaa rog su adhik vadderaa maaeaa kaa muhataaj bheaa |8|
lines.english: The disease of duality is so very deadly; it causes dependence on Maya. ||8||

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾਨਕ ਹਰਿ ਜਨ ਅਨਦਿਨੁ ਨਿਰਮਲ ਜਿਨ ਕਉ ਕਰਮਿ ਨੀਸਾਣੁ ਪਇਆ ॥੯॥੧॥
lines.translit: naanak har jan anadin niramal jin kau karam neesaan peaa |9|1|
lines.english: O Nanak, the humble servant of the Lord is immaculate, night and day; he bears the insignia of the Lord's Grace. ||9||1||

lines.gurmukhi: ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਸਾਚਾ ਸਬਦਿ ਸਲਾਹੈ ਮਨਿ ਸਾਚਾ ਤਿਸੁ ਰੋਗੁ ਗਇਆ ॥
lines.translit: guramukh saachaa sabad salaahai man saachaa tis rog geaa |
lines.english: One who becomes Gurmukh and praises the True Shabad with the True Lord in his mind is cured of his disease.
